category_a.notes: Microcystis aeruginosa; drinking water advisory for 400K people
Microcystin >1 ppb; water system shutdown for 500K people
52 days early detection; spring Mississippi nitrogen loading as precursor
16 days early; HAB confirmed across Chesapeake watershed

category_b.location: Posey Creek, Siskiyou County, CA (NEON POSE)
category_b.notes: 18 days before NEON advisory flag; drought-driven thermal stress

category_b.event_name: Blacktail Deer Creek Storm Conductance Spike (Fall 2024)
category_b.location: Blacktail Deer Creek, Yellowstone NP, WY (NEON BLDE)
category_b.notes: 18 days before routine reporting; mineral/agricultural solute loading

category_b.location: Martha Creek, Niwot LTER, CO (NEON MART)

category_b.notes: 17 days before FL DEP confirmation; hypolimnetic anoxia

category_b.event_name: Le Conte Creek Post-Storm pH Depression (Spring 2024)
category_b.location: Le Conte Creek, Great Smoky Mountains NP, TN (NEON LECO)
category_b.notes: 16 days before NPS field survey; acid flushing depressed pH <6.5
20 days before NPDES identification; fall fertilizer application season

category_c.event_name: Grand Lake St. Marys HAB Crisis (2009–2011)
category_c.location: Grand Lake St. Marys, Mercer/Auglaize Counties, Ohio
category_c.official_advisory: July 2009 no-contact advisory; Distressed Watershed designation Jan 2011
category_c.precursor_signal: TP at 1,660 µg/L, internal P release, phycocyanin rise in May 2009
category_c.source: USGS Scientific Investigations Report 2014-5210; Ohio EPA HAB Response records

category_c.location: Western Lake Erie basin, OH/MI
category_c.official_advisory: July 23, 2015 — Severity Index 10.5 (largest bloom at that time)
category_c.precursor_signal: NOAA GLERL buoys: elevated Chl-a, depressed DO, rising phycocyanin late June
category_c.source: NOAA NCCOS Lake Erie HAB Forecast archive; Ohio Sea Grant records

category_c.location: Lake Okeechobee, south-central Florida
category_c.official_advisory: May 2016 — Florida DOH/DEP recreational advisory; 33 sq mi covered
category_c.precursor_signal: March–April: elevated TP/TN from winter agricultural discharge; DO declining nearshore
category_c.source: USGS OFR 2017-1054; SFWMD sonde data; FAU nutrient study

category_c.event_name: Lake Okeechobee HAB (Summer 2018, State of Emergency)
category_c.location: Lake Okeechobee + Caloosahatchee/St. Lucie Estuaries, FL
category_c.official_advisory: June 2018 — Governor declared state of emergency in 7 counties
category_c.precursor_signal: April 2018: TP above SFWMD thresholds; DO depression in nearshore zones
category_c.source: UF IFAS Extension FAQ on 2018 algae blooms; USGS Lake Okeechobee bloom dynamics

category_c.event_name: San Francisco Bay Heterosigma Fish Kill (August 2022)
category_c.location: San Francisco Bay (South Bay, Oakland Estuary, San Pablo Bay), CA
category_c.official_advisory: August 22–25, 2022 — DO <2 mg/L; mass fish kill (sturgeon, sharks, striped bass)
category_c.precursor_signal: SFEI sonde network: DO declining ~July 25 (27 days before lethal levels)
category_c.source: CA State Water Board press release Aug 2022; USGS CWSC Heterosigma study PMC70273277

category_c.location: Klamath River, Copco/J.C. Boyle Reservoirs, CA/OR border
category_c.official_advisory: September 10, 2021 — full lake advisory (910 ppb microcystin; J.C. Boyle 18 ppb)
category_c.precursor_signal: Mid-July: Karuk/Yurok tribal monitoring: Microcystis at monitoring stations; DO declining
category_c.source: Oregon Health Authority advisory records; Klamath Tribes cyanobacteria page

category_c.location: Utah Lake, Utah County, Utah
category_c.official_advisory: June 2016 — unprecedented full-lake closure; 100+ illness reports
category_c.precursor_signal: May 2016: Utah DEQ continuous monitoring: TP/TN rising, phycocyanin up, temp >20°C
category_c.source: University of Utah CEE HAB report; Utah DEQ HAB archives

category_c.location: Utah Lake, Lincoln Beach and east shore, Utah
category_c.official_advisory: June 30, 2018 — Lincoln Beach closure and 'Danger' advisory
category_c.precursor_signal: June 20: Dolichospermum measurable; June 25: 14× higher (5 days before closure)
category_c.source: Salt Lake Tribune June 30, 2018; Utah DEQ recreational monitoring archives

category_c.location: Lower Mississippi River, Orleans/Plaquemines Parishes, Louisiana
category_c.official_advisory: September 22, 2023 — Plaquemines Parish communities lost potable water
category_c.precursor_signal: Late July: USGS continuous SpCond at >10 Lower Mississippi gauges showing chloride rise
category_c.source: NBC News Oct 2023; GRL doi:10.1029/2024GL108545; Drought.gov Lower Mississippi dashboard

category_c.location: Delaware River Estuary, Philadelphia region / southern NJ
category_c.official_advisory: DRBC salt front approaching 1.4M-person intake; formal warnings issued
category_c.precursor_signal: Summer 2022: DRBC 82-station SpCond network tracking upstream salt front migration
category_c.source: DRBC Salt Front page; USGS NGWOS Delaware River Basin; DRBC Dec 2025 technical report

category_c.event_name: Animas River AMD Chronic Buildup (2015, pre-Gold King)
category_c.location: Animas River / Cement Creek, Silverton to Farmington, CO/NM
category_c.official_advisory: August 5, 2015 (acute release); chronic buildup detectable weeks prior
category_c.precursor_signal: pH 3.5–4.5 and metal loading rising in Cement Creek through spring/summer 2015
category_c.note: SENTINEL applicable for CHRONIC trend only, not the acute August 5 release
category_c.source: USGS Gold King Mine release page; EPA Emergency Response page; PMC5568564

category_c.event_name: Neuse River Estuary Hypoxia / Fish Kill (2020–2022)
category_c.location: Neuse River Estuary, eastern North Carolina
category_c.official_advisory: Summer each year: NC DEQ fish kill reports; September 2023 formal monitoring
category_c.precursor_signal: May–June: bottom DO declining; NC State hypoxia forecast model predicts 6 weeks ahead
category_c.source: NC Sea Grant Coastwatch Currents June 2022; NC DEQ press release Sept 2023

category_c.location: Jordan Lake State Recreation Area, Chatham/Wake Counties, NC
category_c.official_advisory: July–Aug each year: NC DEQ recreational advisory when toxins exceed thresholds
category_c.precursor_signal: April–May: UNC Jordan Lake Observatory DO diurnal extremes (DO afternoon >130%, night <70%)
category_c.source: PMC5848192; USGS SAWSC stratification study; Jordan Lake Observatory (jordanlakeobservatory.unc.edu)

category_c.location: Raccoon River and Des Moines River, central Iowa
category_c.official_advisory: 2015: record 177 operating days of denitrification at DMWW ($1.5M cost)
category_c.precursor_signal: March–April: USGS NWIS 05482000 shows nitrate rising 3–4 weeks before MCL exceedance
category_c.source: DMWW 2015 Denitrification Record; Iowa Public Radio; USGS NWIS 05482000

category_c.location: Chesapeake Bay mainstem, Maryland/Virginia
category_c.official_advisory: Late June 2018: Maryland DNR hypoxia cruise confirmed above-average volume
category_c.precursor_signal: Jan–May 2018: Susquehanna USGS stations show above-average spring N loading (90 days ahead)
category_c.source: Maryland DNR Late July 2018 Hypoxia Report; USGS PA Water Science Center; CBP Oct 23, 2018

category_c.location: Southern Green Bay, Lake Michigan, Wisconsin
category_c.official_advisory: NCCOS-confirmed persistent summer hypoxia; DO <5 mg/L across ~500–600 km²
category_c.precursor_signal: May–June each year: MMSD 23-year continuous DO monitoring (15-min intervals) tracks decline
category_c.source: ScienceDirect doi:10.1016/j.jglr.2018...; NOAA NCCOS Green Bay Dead Zone project

category_c.location: Saginaw Bay, Lake Huron, Michigan
category_c.official_advisory: Mid-July each year: NOAA cyanobacterial index triggers bloom-level detection
category_c.precursor_signal: Late June/early July: NOAA GLERL/CIGLR biweekly sampling detects early Microcystis cells 14–21d ahead
category_c.source: NOAA NCEI GLERL-CIGLR-HAB-SagBay; GLERL blog July 2024; NCCOS satellite monitoring

category_c.location: Hudson River Estuary, Kingston to Norrie Point, New York (60-mile reach)
category_c.official_advisory: September 10, 2025 — Cary Institute reported; DEC issued advisories for affected reach
category_c.precursor_signal: July–Aug 2025: DEC/Cary monitoring elevated nutrients; rising temps; phytoplankton shifts
category_c.source: Cary Institute press release Sept 10, 2025; NY DEC HAB Roadmap March 2026; NYSDEC 2025 factsheet

category_c.location: Clear Lake, Lake County, California
category_c.official_advisory: July 2, 2024 — 'Danger' advisory for Lower Arm and Oaks Arm (OEHHA threshold exceeded)
category_c.precursor_signal: May–June 2024: UC Davis hourly sensors show DO anomalies and phytoplankton fluorescence rising
category_c.source: Gridley Herald July 2, 2024; CA Water Boards Clear Lake Nutrient Control Program; 2026 GeoHealth paper

category_c.event_name: Tar Creek AMD — Tri-State Mining District (chronic)
category_c.location: Tar Creek, Ottawa County, northeastern Oklahoma (EPA Superfund)
category_c.official_advisory: Continuous Superfund exceedances; cadmium 8×, lead 19×, zinc 83× over OK standards
category_c.precursor_signal: Rainfall events: pH drops within 24–72h; SpCond is continuous AMD loading indicator (1M gal/day discharge)
category_c.source: EPA Superfund Site ID 0601269; USGS Picher mining area chemical analyses; USGS Mine Drainage page

category_c.event_name: Lake Winnebago / Winnebago Pool HAB (recurring annual)
category_c.location: Lake Winnebago and Winnebago Pool, Wisconsin
category_c.official_advisory: Wisconsin DNR blue-green algae advisories each summer (drinking water source for 4 cities)
category_c.precursor_signal: May–June: NOAA satellite cyanobacterial index detects rising signal; USGS tracks Fox/Wolf River P loading
category_c.source: NOAA NCCOS satellite monitoring; USGS Winnebago pool lakes study; Wisconsin DNR Blue-Green Algae page

live_crises.action: AquaSSM can ingest SFWMD live sensor streams. HydroViT phycocyanin proxy can monitor bloom extent.
live_crises.source: DOH-Palm Beach advisory March 20, 2026; SFWMD Algal Bloom Dashboard

live_crises.location: Raccoon River and Des Moines River, Iowa
live_crises.action: AquaSSM spring nitrate loading model can provide 3–4 week advance warning for DMWW treatment capacity.
live_crises.source: Iowa Capital Dispatch Dec 2025; US News April 1, 2026; DMWW Stage III protocols

live_crises.action: 90-day spring nitrogen loading precursor already detectable via Susquehanna USGS stations.
live_crises.source: Maryland DNR Dec 3, 2025 report; VIMS 2025 Chesapeake dead zone assessment

live_crises.crisis_name: Gulf of Mexico Hypoxia (2026 Season, May Onset)
live_crises.location: Northern Gulf of Mexico (mouth of Mississippi/Atchafalaya)
live_crises.action: Spring 2026 Mississippi loading data already available. SENTINEL can forecast dead zone size 60–90 days ahead.
live_crises.source: NOAA NCCOS 'Below Average Summer 2025 Dead Zone'; Friends of the Mississippi River 2025 report

live_crises.location: Nationwide (9,728 confirmed sites as of March 2026)
live_crises.action: NOVEL USE CASE: Standard sensors cannot detect PFAS. MicroBiomeNet microbiome shifts and ToxiGene transcriptomic patterns can serve as indirect PFAS biomarkers.
live_crises.source: EPA UCMR 5 press release March 2026; MDPI Gunpowder River PFAS study Jan 2026; EWG map

data_sources: UC Davis Clear Lake 5-year hourly sensors
live_crises.action: 2026 GeoHealth paper provides statewide validation dataset. Clear Lake's hourly AIS-style data is ideal for AquaSSM.
live_crises.source: GeoHealth doi:10.1029/2025GH001627; CA Water Boards SWAMP CyanoHABs; UC Davis Clear Lake monitoring

data_sources: NY DEC continuous water quality network
live_crises.action: DEC HAB Roadmap (March 2026) creates direct policy partnership opportunity. MicroBiomeNet can characterize bloom microbiome. 2025 patterns inform 2026 early warning threshold calibration.
live_crises.source: Cary Institute Sept 2025; NY DEC HAB Roadmap March 2026; NYSDEC 2025 factsheet
